# GitHub repository setup

Set these on the repo’s **About** section (click the gear next to “About” on the repo homepage) so the repo is easy to find and clearly tied to Bytesflows.

---

## Repository description

Copy-paste into the **Description** field (or shorten if needed):

```
Guides and articles on web scraping and dynamic residential proxies. Published at bytesflows.com
```

Shorter option:

```
Web scraping guides & dynamic residential proxy docs · bytesflows.com
```

---

## Topics (tags)

Add these **Topics** so the repo appears in GitHub topic search and on topic pages. In About → Topics, add:

- `web-scraping`
- `residential-proxies`
- `proxy`
- `scraping`
- `playwright`
- `python`
- `blog`
- `bytesflows`

You can add more (e.g. `cloudflare`, `scrapy`, `crawler`) if they fit. Keep the list readable (about 8–12 topics).

---

## Website and optional links

In About, set **Website** to:

```
https://bytesflows.com
```

Optionally use **Topics** and **Description** only; the README already links to the blog and proxies.
